title: ranunculus garganicus proles canutii
intro: Ranunculus garganicus proles canutii, also known as lesser celandine or pilewort, is a perennial plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of ranunculin, a toxic alkaloid. The health benefits of Ranunculus garganicus proles canutii include its potential to treat various conditions such as eczema, acne, and even cancer, due to its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into a tincture or ointment, which can be applied topically to the affected area, although it's essential to note that excessive consumption can be toxic and should be avoided.
